baseline_v0.summary: GPT with flash attention 3, RoPE, RMSNorm, value residuals (ResFormer), sliding window attention (SSSL pattern), squared ReLU MLP, softcap 15 logits, Muon+AdamW hybrid optimizer. 8 layers, aspect ratio 64, head dim 128.

mamba.summary:
Mamba replaces the quadratic-time attention layer with a *selective* structured state-space model (S4) whose Δ, B, C parameters are input-dependent functions instead of fixed tensors.  
This single change lets the model gate tokens along the sequence dimension (content-aware forgetting/remembering) while still admitting a linear-time, hardware-friendly GPU scan: parameters stay in fast SRAM, a parallel-scan kernel fuses the recurrence, and intermediate states are recomputed during backprop, so memory stays O(BLD) like FlashAttention.  
The resulting architecture is a homogeneous stack of these selective-SSM blocks—no attention heads, no MLPs—yielding Transformer-quality perplexity on language, audio, and genomics with 5× faster autoregressive generation and linear scaling to million-token contexts.

rwkv.summary:
RWKV replaces quadratic self-attention with a *linear* attention mechanism that reformulates the QK^T matrix as a *channel-wise* vector decaying exponentially with relative position.  
This decay vector (W) is trained once per channel and applied via a custom CUDA kernel, turning the attention update into an RNN-style recurrence wkv_t = Σ_{i≤t} e^{-w(t-i)} k_i v_i that can be parallel-scanned during training yet needs only O(d) state at inference.  

The architecture keeps the Transformer’s training parallelizability (time-parallel mode) while giving RNN-level memory (O(d)) and time (O(Td)) at inference; a “token-shift” operator blends current and previous hidden states to inject local context without convolution.  

Unlike standard Transformers, RWKV is *formulated as both* a Transformer (for fast batched training) and an RNN (for constant-state autoregressive generation), eliminating the quadratic bottleneck without low-rank or sparsity approximations and scaling to 14 B parameters with matched performance.

retnet.summary:
RetNet replaces softmax attention with a **retention mechanism** that has **dual parallel and recurrent forms**.  
During training the parallel form keeps GPU-friendly matrix multiplications; at inference the recurrent form updates a fixed-size hidden state in **O(1) time and memory**, eliminating the KV-cache and making latency independent of sequence length.  
A **chunkwise recurrent** variant splits long inputs into local blocks processed in parallel while summarising cross-chunk information recurrently, yielding **linear overall complexity** and constant activation memory.  
Each head uses its own **exponential decay factor γ** applied to a relative-position matrix **D**; this multi-scale decay plus a **Swish gate** and **GroupNorm** gives the model both position-aware and content-aware credit assignment without the quadratic attention matrix.  
The result is one architecture that simultaneously offers **training parallelism, Transformer-level perplexity, and O(1) inference cost**, breaking the “impossible triangle” that limited prior linear/RNN/SSM alternatives.

hyena.summary:
Hyena replaces the quadratic-cost attention matrix with a data-controlled, subquadratic operator built from two primitives repeated in depth: implicit long convolutions (filters generated on-the-fly by a small FFN) and element-wise gating.  
This yields an O(L log L) recurrence that factorizes a dense, input-dependent linear map without ever materializing an L×L matrix, giving unrestricted context like attention while keeping parameters decoupled from sequence length.  
Unlike prior low-rank/sparse approximations, Hyena needs no hybrid attention layers to match Transformer quality, scaling to 64k-token sequences with 100× speed-ups over FlashAttention and setting the first dense-attention-free SOTA on WikiText103/The Pile.

griffin.summary:
Griffin replaces the quadratic-cost global attention in Transformers with a hybrid temporal-mixer that alternates a novel Real-Gated Linear Recurrent Unit (RG-LRU) with local sliding-window attention.  
RG-LRU keeps a fixed-size hidden state updated by element-wise *gated* diagonal recurrences (input gate *iₜ* and recurrence gate *rₜ* modulate a learnable decay *a*), eliminating the KV cache growth that plagues Transformers while still allowing gradient-parallel training.  
By mixing these cheap recurrences with a 1024-token local attention every few layers, Griffin retains the ability to model both distant dependencies (via recurrence) and fine-grained local context (via attention) without ever materialising attention over the full sequence.  
The resulting 14 B-parameter model matches Llama-2 quality after 6× fewer training tokens, trains at Transformer speed on TPUs via a custom Pallas kernel that fuses the memory-bound recurrence, and at inference delivers 2–3× higher throughput and lower latency on long sequences because its cache stays constant-size while the Transformer’s KV cache grows linearly.

xlstm.summary:
xLSTM replaces the original LSTM’s sigmoid gates with exponential gating plus a normalizer state, enabling the network to revise stored values and stabilizing very large gate magnitudes.  
It introduces two new cells: sLSTM keeps a scalar memory but adds “memory-mixing” across multiple cells/heads inside each head, while mLSTM replaces the scalar cell with a d×d matrix updated by an outer-product (covariance) rule, giving linear-time parallel computation and far larger storage.  
Both cells are dropped into modern residual blocks (pre- or post-upprojection) and stacked like Transformers, yielding an architecture that is constant-memory in sequence length yet matches or beats strong Transformer/SSM baselines in language-modeling perplexity and long-context extrapolation.

gla.summary:
The paper introduces FLASHLINEARATTENTION, an I/O-aware chunkwise algorithm that fuses operations and keeps tiles in SRAM, making linear attention faster than FlashAttention-2 even at 1K sequences.  
It augments the linear RNN with a data-dependent vector-valued gate αt that modulates the 2-D hidden state via Hadamard product Gt = αt 1T, giving the model a fine-grained forgetting mechanism while still admitting a stable chunkwise parallel form.  
A two-level tiling scheme is proposed: inter-sub-chunk interactions use fast half-precision matmuls on tensor cores, while intra-sub-chunk attention-style weights are computed in full-precision log space, balancing speed and numerical stability.  
The resulting Gated Linear Attention (GLA) layer keeps parameter count comparable to softmax attention (≈ 4d²), supports linear-time generation with enlarged matrix states, and scales to 24K-context training via truncated BPTT, outperforming RetNet/Mamba on recall tasks and length extrapolation while maintaining higher training throughput.
